canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, TinyStories is consistently described as a dataset of synthetic, short, and simple stories generated by large language models, with some sources specifying GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 as the generators. Its most common stated purpose is to facilitate the training and analysis of language models, with one paper noting it "aims to represent key challenges in text generation while keeping training tractable for small to medium scale models" (The Belief State Transformer). The dataset is primarily used to measure or evaluate the capabilities of `Text generation` and `Language modeling`. Beyond these general applications, some work uses TinyStories for more specific probes, such as examining "character choices" in LLMs (Emergent Response Planning in LLMs). In practice, it is sometimes combined with other corpora, like "Children Stories," for training models on language generation tasks. The definitions are largely in agreement, emphasizing the dataset's synthetic origin and its design for tractable yet challenging model training.
papers: Bilinear MLPs enable weight-based mechanistic interpretability
SWIFT: On-the-Fly Self-Speculative Decoding for LLM Inference Acceleration
An Analysis for Reasoning Bias of Language Models with Small Initialization
Memorization Sinks: Isolating Memorization during LLM Training
On Understanding Attention-Based In-Context Learning for Categorical Data

definitions.text: A dataset of synthetic short stories generated by GPT-3.5 and GPT-4, designed to be small enough for training tractable models while still presenting challenges in text generation like coherence and narrative structure.
A dataset composed of short, simple stories generated by a large language model, used for training and analyzing language generation capabilities.
definitions.paper: On Understanding Attention-Based In-Context Learning for Categorical Data

relationships.paper: On the Power of Decision Trees in Auto-Regressive Language Modeling
relationships.evidence: We conducted experiments using LLaMA-2-13B across the CNN/Daily Mail (Nallapati et al., 2016), GSM8K (Cobbe et al., 2021), and TinyStories (Eldan & Li, 2023) datasets. (Section 3.2.1)
